steps.title: Apply the Cone Volume Formula
steps.content:
For a cone with radius $r = 6$ cm and height $h = 13$ cm, substitute these values:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (6)^2 (13)$$
Evaluate $(6)^2 = 36$.
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (36)(13)$$

steps.title: Simplify to Find the Volume
steps.content:
Multiply the integers: $36 \times 13 = 468$.
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (468) = 156\pi$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
